channel.title: Arcbotix
channel.description: Practical robotics engineering guides and niche technical deep-dives

item.title: Sliding Mode Control for Robot Arm Joints: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Sliding mode control for a robot arm joint explained with real numbers: sizing the sliding surface, the switching gain, and the boundary layer that kills chattering.

item.title: Ball Screw vs Lead Screw for a Robot Linear Actuator: A Worked Torque Sizing Example
item.description: Ball screw vs lead screw for a robot linear actuator, with a full worked torque sizing example for a SCARA Z-axis lift, including holding torque and backdrive behavior.

item.title: Tool Center Point (TCP) Calibration for Robot Arms: A Worked Pivot Calibration Example
item.description: TCP calibration for robot arms explained with an actual worked pivot-calibration example: the least-squares math behind the offset, not just the procedure.

item.title: RS-485/Modbus for Robot Joint Networks: Wiring, Addressing, and Polling-Rate Budget
item.description: RS-485/Modbus robot joint network wiring, addressing, and the frame-time math that determines how fast you can actually poll N daisy-chained joint drivers.

item.title: EtherCAT for Robot Joint Networks: How the Cycle-Time Budget Actually Works
item.description: EtherCAT for robot joint networks explained with a worked cycle-time budget: frame transmission time, per-slave forwarding delay, and cable propagation.

item.title: Zero Moment Point for Legged Robot Balance: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Learn the zero moment point (ZMP) stability criterion for legged robots with a worked numeric example: computing ZMP location and checking it against the support polygon.

item.title: Series Elastic Actuator Design: Sizing the Spring Stiffness for a Robot Joint
item.description: A worked example for sizing series elastic actuator spring stiffness: bandwidth, peak torque, and deflection-to-torque calibration for a robot joint.

item.title: Homing a Robot Arm Joint with a Limit Switch: The Encoder-Offset Math
item.description: How to home a robot arm joint with a limit switch: mounting, the approach/back-off sequence, and a worked encoder-offset calculation with real numbers.

item.title: Sensorless BLDC Motor Commutation: A Worked Back-EMF Zero-Crossing Timing Example
item.description: Sensorless BLDC motor commutation explained with real numbers: the electrical period, the 30-degree phase-lag delay in microseconds, and the low-speed blanking-time failure mode.

item.title: BLDC Hall Sensor Commutation Explained: Building the Six-Step Table from Scratch
item.description: BLDC hall sensor commutation explained from first principles: the six hall states, the electrical-angle math behind the switching table, and portable C code.

item.title: ROS2 tf2 Transforms Explained: A Worked Example (and Why Lookups Fail)
item.description: ROS2 tf2 transforms explained with a worked homogeneous transform example and the real reasons lookupTransform throws extrapolation and frame-not-found errors.

item.title: Emergency Stop Circuit Design for a Robot Arm: Category 3 Dual-Channel Wiring Explained
item.description: A worked Category 3 e-stop circuit design example for a robot arm: dual-channel wiring, cross-fault detection, and a stop-response time budget with real numbers.

item.title: Force Sensing in Robot Grippers: A Worked Load Cell Calibration and PID Force Control Example
item.description: Load cell force sensing for robot grippers explained: bridge output to amplifier gain to ADC counts to grams, then a worked PID force control example.

item.title: Motor and Gearbox Sizing for a Robot Arm Joint: A Worked Torque Calculation
item.description: Motor and gearbox sizing for a robot arm joint explained with a full worked example: load torque, safety factor, gear ratio choice, and a continuous-torque check.

item.title: Robot Arm Joint Friction Compensation: A Worked Coulomb and Viscous Example
item.description: Learn robot arm joint friction compensation with a worked Coulomb and viscous friction example: measure coefficients, add a feedforward term, fix stick-slip.

item.title: Absolute vs Incremental Encoders for Robot Joints: How to Size the Resolution You Actually Need
item.description: Absolute vs incremental encoder for robot joints, explained with a worked example: converting a target joint accuracy into encoder bits, PPR, and gear ratio.

item.title: Impedance Control vs Admittance Control for Robot Arms: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Impedance control vs admittance control explained with a worked one-DOF mass-spring-damper example, real numbers, and which one fits your robot arm hardware.

item.title: CAN Bus for Robot Joint Networks: Wiring, Bit Timing, and Bus Load Explained
item.description: A worked example of CAN bus for robot joint networks: termination placement, bit-timing calculation for cable length, and bus-load arithmetic for a 6-joint arm.

item.title: Computed Torque Control for Robot Arms: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Computed torque control explained with real numbers: canceling a two-link arm's nonlinear dynamics and reducing tracking error to a simple double integrator.

item.title: Real-Time Considerations in Robot Control Loops: Budgeting Jitter and Choosing Your Loop Rate
item.description: Real-time considerations in robot control loops explained with a jitter budget formula, timer code, and the gain margin math that connects timing to stability.
